evals.prompt: Set up CI/CD for our Node API so pushes to main run tests, build a Docker image, push to GHCR, and deploy to production with rollback guidance.
evals.expected_output: Chooses a container-service deployment lane, keeps one immutable artifact, and includes release-safety guidance instead of jumping to unrelated infra.
assertions: Response identifies the workload, artifact, and hosting lane before listing commands
Response requires immutable tags or versions instead of relying on latest only
Response mentions health checks or rollback guidance

evals.prompt: We have a Next.js marketing site with preview environments. Should we use Vercel, Cloud Run, or Kubernetes?
evals.expected_output: Prefers the smallest viable deployment surface and explains why platform hosting may be better than custom orchestration.
assertions: Response compares deployment surfaces instead of defaulting to Kubernetes
Response explains why a platform-hosted lane is likely sufficient for this workload
Response routes direct Vercel shipping requests toward the narrower vercel-deploy skill when appropriate

evals.prompt: Review this deployment workflow: it SSHes into production, stores secrets in workflow YAML, and deploys latest tags only.
evals.expected_output: Flags the unsafe parts, proposes a safer promotion path, and keeps the review focused on release risk.
assertions: Response flags secrets in source or workflow YAML as unsafe
Response proposes a safer rollout or rollback structure

evals.prompt: Design a zero-downtime rollout for a payments worker that processes long-running jobs during deploys.
evals.expected_output: Matches rollout strategy to workload risk and covers shutdown, health, and recovery concerns.
assertions: Response treats graceful shutdown or job-drain behavior as part of the rollout design
Response chooses rolling, blue-green, or canary based on workload risk instead of by default
Response includes verification or monitoring steps for the rollout
